questions.question_text: Which of the following terms best describes the harmful, excessive or non-medical use of psychoactive substances by an individual?
questions.explanation: Drug abuse refers to the harmful, excessive or non-medical use of psychoactive substances that produces dependence, adverse physical effects and social problems for the individual and society.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Prescription is medical authorisation, not misuse.
Diffusion describes movement of particles, not drug use.
Synthesis refers to chemical production of drugs.

questions.question_text: Which international body is primarily responsible for formulating and maintaining the global Prohibited List of substances banned in sport?
questions.explanation: WADA, the World Anti-Doping Agency founded in 1999, publishes the Prohibited List annually and coordinates worldwide anti-doping policy, testing standards and compliance monitoring across sports.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: IOC governs Olympics but delegates doping policy to WADA.
FIFA governs football only and follows WADA rules.
UNESCO supports the anti-doping convention but does not make the list.

questions.question_text: Stimulants are a class of banned drugs in sport because they primarily produce which of the following physiological effects on the athlete?

options.text: Slow heart rate and deep sedation
Increase alertness, heart rate and reduce perceived fatigue
Depress respiration and cause muscle relaxation
Block pain signals without affecting the brain

questions.explanation: Stimulants such as amphetamines, cocaine and ephedrine excite the central nervous system, raise heart rate and blood pressure, increase alertness and mask fatigue, giving an unfair performance edge.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Slow heart rate and sedation are effects of depressants.
Respiratory depression is caused by narcotics, not stimulants.
Pain blocking without CNS action describes local anaesthetics.

questions.question_text: Narcotic analgesics are prohibited in sports mainly because they allow athletes to do which of the following during competition?

options.text: Mask pain and continue playing through serious injury

questions.explanation: Narcotic analgesics like morphine and pethidine suppress pain perception, letting athletes ignore injury signals and worsen damage; they also cause dependence and respiratory depression, hence they are banned.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Lung capacity is not altered by narcotic analgesics.
Muscle building is the effect of anabolic agents.
Coordination is typically impaired, not improved, by narcotics.

questions.question_text: The National Anti-Doping Agency (NADA) of India was established in the year 2005 primarily to carry out which of the following functions?

options.text: Promote alcohol awareness among school children
Implement the WADA anti-doping code within India
Conduct talent scouting for Olympic Games

questions.explanation: NADA, set up in 2005 under the Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, implements the WADA Code in India through dope testing, result management, education and promotion of clean sport.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: School alcohol programmes are run by health, not sports agencies.
NADA does not manufacture supplements or nutritional products.
Talent scouting is handled by SAI and federations, not NADA.

questions.question_text: The 'whereabouts rule' in the anti-doping programme requires elite athletes in a Registered Testing Pool to do which of the following?

options.text: Submit monthly medical bills to NADA
Provide quarterly updates on location and a 60-minute daily testing slot
Stay in national camps at all times
Disclose their personal bank account details

questions.explanation: Under the whereabouts rule, athletes in the Registered Testing Pool file quarterly information about their overnight address, training and competition schedule, plus a daily 60-minute slot for no-advance-notice out-of-competition testing.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Medical bills are not part of whereabouts requirements.
Athletes can train privately; camp residence is not mandated.
Bank details are unrelated to anti-doping testing logistics.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is the most accurate description of the long-term effect of chronic alcohol consumption on the human liver?

options.text: Increased regeneration of hepatocytes and faster healing
Fatty liver progressing to hepatitis and eventually cirrhosis
Complete immunity to viral infections of the liver
Permanent enlargement of bile ducts only

questions.explanation: Chronic heavy drinking typically follows a pathway of fatty liver (steatosis) to alcoholic hepatitis and finally irreversible cirrhosis, where scar tissue replaces healthy hepatocytes and liver function fails.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Alcohol damages hepatocytes rather than promoting regeneration.
Alcohol use actually increases risk of viral hepatitis progression.
Bile duct enlargement alone does not capture cirrhosis pathway.

questions.question_text: An endurance cyclist uses recombinant erythropoietin (rhEPO) before a major race; which category of the WADA Prohibited List does this substance primarily belong to?
questions.explanation: Recombinant EPO is a peptide hormone that stimulates red blood cell production, thereby boosting oxygen-carrying capacity; WADA lists it under S2 Peptide Hormones, Growth Factors, Related Substances and Mimetics.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Narcotic analgesics suppress pain, not red cell production.
Beta-blockers reduce tremor; they are banned only in precision sports.
Diuretics flush fluids and mask drugs; EPO is not a masking agent.

questions.question_text: A shooter found using a beta-blocker before an event faces sanctions because beta-blockers are banned in certain sports for which specific reason?

options.text: They build lean muscle mass rapidly
They reduce tremor and steady the hand for precision
They accelerate recovery from muscular injury

questions.explanation: Beta-blockers lower heart rate and suppress adrenaline-driven tremor, giving an unfair advantage in precision sports like shooting, archery, golf and billiards; hence WADA bans them in those disciplines.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Muscle building is the effect of anabolic steroids.
Beta-blockers in fact lower cardiac output and endurance.
Recovery from injury is not the mechanism targeted here.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is the most comprehensive reason why performance-enhancing drugs are banned in organised competitive sport worldwide?

options.text: They are expensive for athletes to purchase
They threaten athlete health, violate fair play and damage the spirit of sport
They are difficult to store in cold climates
They cannot be produced in sufficient quantities

questions.explanation: Doping is banned because it endangers athlete health, creates unfair competition, undermines the ethical spirit of sport captured in the WADA Code, and sets harmful role models for young people who emulate top athletes.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Cost alone is not a reason for international prohibition.
Storage difficulty is a logistic, not ethical, concern.
Production capacity is irrelevant to the ban rationale.

questions.question_text: Match the drug class in Column A with the corresponding representative example or primary effect listed in Column B, then choose the correct mapping.
questions.explanation: Stimulants include amphetamine/ephedrine; narcotic analgesics include morphine/pethidine; anabolic agents include testosterone/nandrolone; diuretics include furosemide/acetazolamide used as masking agents.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Testosterone and nandrolone are anabolic, never stimulants.
Furosemide is a diuretic, not a narcotic.
Morphine is a narcotic analgesic, not a diuretic.

questions.question_text: Match each anti-doping entity or term in Column A with its correct role or meaning from Column B and select the option showing the right mapping.

column_b.text: Database for whereabouts and test results

questions.explanation: WADA is the global anti-doping authority; NADA is its Indian counterpart; TUE permits medically necessary banned medication; ADAMS is the Anti-Doping Administration and Management System database used worldwide.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: WADA is global, so cannot be the Indian agency.
TUE is a medical exemption, not a database.
ADAMS stores data; it is not an agency name.

questions.question_text: Match each harmful effect in Column A with the primary substance typically responsible for it in Column B, and choose the correct combination below.
questions.explanation: Smoking causes lung cancer and bronchitis; alcohol leads to cirrhosis; anabolic steroids cause aggression and testicular shrinkage; opioids depress respiration and produce strong physical dependence with tolerance.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Lung cancer is overwhelmingly tobacco-related.
Cirrhosis is classically linked to alcohol.
Testicular atrophy is a hallmark of anabolic steroid abuse.

questions.question_text: Read the assertion and reason carefully and choose the option that best describes their logical relationship in the context of anti-doping.
questions.assertion: Anabolic androgenic steroids are prohibited in sport at all times, both in and out of competition.
questions.reason: They promote protein synthesis and muscle growth, giving athletes an unfair long-term structural advantage.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true; Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both are true but Reason does not correctly explain Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

questions.explanation: Anabolic steroids are S1 non-specified substances banned at all times precisely because their muscle-building effects persist well beyond the period of use, making out-of-competition testing essential.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: The reason given does explain the year-round prohibition.
Both statements are factually correct.
Option D is incorrect because the assertion is clearly true.

questions.question_text: Examine the assertion and reason about tobacco use and pick the choice that correctly describes their logical connection.
questions.assertion: Smoking tobacco significantly reduces exercise capacity and endurance in athletes.
questions.reason: Carbon monoxide in tobacco smoke binds haemoglobin more strongly than oxygen, reducing oxygen delivery to muscles.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true; Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both are true but Reason does not correctly explain Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

questions.explanation: Carbon monoxide has roughly 200 times the affinity of oxygen for haemoglobin, forming carboxyhaemoglobin; this limits oxygen transport during exercise and directly explains reduced endurance in smokers.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: The reason is a textbook physiological fact.
The assertion is well-established by exercise studies.
Carbon monoxide's haemoglobin binding directly causes capacity loss.

questions.question_text: Consider the following assertion and reason about the WADA Strict Liability principle and select the correct interpretation.
questions.assertion: Under the Strict Liability principle, an athlete is responsible for any prohibited substance found in their body regardless of intent.
questions.reason: WADA assumes that all athletes deliberately plan every doping violation in advance of competition.

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are true; Reason correctly explains Assertion
Both are true but Reason does not correctly explain Assertion
Assertion is true but Reason is false
Assertion is false but Reason is true

questions.explanation: Strict liability holds athletes accountable for substances in their sample even without proof of intent, to protect clean sport; however WADA does not assume deliberate planning—intent affects sanction length only.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: The assertion about liability regardless of intent is accurate.
Intent is actually separated from liability in the Code.
WADA does not presume universal deliberate doping.

questions.question_text: Arrange the standard steps of the in-competition urine sample collection procedure followed by a Doping Control Officer in the correct chronological order.

items.text: Athlete selects a sealed collection vessel
Notification of selection and escorting to Doping Control Station
Splitting of sample into sealed A and B bottles
Provision of urine sample under observation

questions.explanation: The DCO first notifies and escorts the athlete, who then selects a sealed vessel, provides the sample under direct observation, and finally splits it into A and B bottles which are sealed and signed.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Notification logically precedes vessel selection.
Sample provision cannot occur before vessel selection.
Splitting into A and B bottles is always the final step.

questions.question_text: Arrange the following stages of the Anti-Doping Rule Violation results-management process in the correct chronological order as per the WADA Code.

items.text: B-sample analysis if athlete requests it
A-sample analysis reports Adverse Analytical Finding
Hearing by Anti-Doping Disciplinary Panel and sanction

questions.explanation: The laboratory first reports an Adverse Analytical Finding from the A-sample; the athlete is notified and provisionally suspended, may request B-sample confirmation, and finally faces a hearing with possible sanction.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Analysis precedes any notification or hearing.
Provisional suspension always comes before hearing.
B-sample is an optional intermediate step, not first.

questions.question_text: State whether the following statement is true or false: Caffeine is currently included on the WADA Prohibited List of banned substances in competition.
questions.explanation: Caffeine was removed from the WADA Prohibited List in 2004 and now sits on the Monitoring Programme instead; athletes may consume it but WADA tracks usage patterns across sports.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Caffeine is legal in coffee, tea and many supplements.
It is only on the Monitoring List, not prohibited.
No sanction follows from positive caffeine tests today.

questions.question_text: Study the diagram showing the WADA Prohibited List categories with their codes and scope; then identify which category primarily includes anabolic androgenic steroids banned at all times.

options.text: S1 — Anabolic Agents (banned at all times)

questions.explanation: The WADA Prohibited List places anabolic androgenic steroids such as testosterone and nandrolone in category S1, which is banned both in and out of competition because the performance benefits persist long after use.
questions.subject: Physical Education

elimination_hints.hint: Stimulants (S6) are only banned in competition.
Narcotics (S7) likewise apply in-competition only.
Beta-blockers (P1) are restricted to precision sports.
